title: Gonjang Monastery: Historical Foundation & Nyingma Jangter Lineage Documents (1981)
description: Founding history by H.E. *Tingkye Gonjang Rinpoche* (1981) and its connection to the 15th-century *Tertön* lineage of Yolmo Terton Ngakchang Shakya Zangpo. Documents detail the continuity of the *'hidden-treasure' (Jangter) tradition* in modern Sikkim. Includes reference to the 19th-century Tibetan precursor, 'Gonjang Samten Choephug'.

title: Gonjang Monastery: Iconography and Mural Catalogue - Main Shrine Hall
description: High-resolution digital archive of the large *Buddha statue, the statues of the **Khen-lop Cho-Sum* (Three Founding Fathers of Tibetan Buddhism), and the engraved statues of the *Je-Bang Nyernga* (Twenty-Five Disciples of Padmasambhava). Detailed images of the intricate and colourful interior murals and door-frames.

title: Gonjang Monastery: Unique Nyingma Ritual Artefacts Collection
description: Catalogue of *unique religious artefacts* housed at the monastery, including probable ritual implements (*vajra, bell, dorje, phurba*), specialized thangka paintings, prayer wheels, and other objects specific to the Nyingma Order's tradition.

title: Gonjang Monastery: Monastic Education (Nine-Year Curriculum) & Community Services
description: Curriculum details for the *nine-year monastic course* covering Tibetan language, English, moral values, and comparative study of *Indian and Tibetan Buddhist philosophy. Includes operational records of the **Thadrol Dorjee Memorial Community Health Care Centre* which serves monks and local community members.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Rare Ati-Buddha Statue Documentation (Yab-Yum Position)
description: Detailed study and high-resolution imagery of the monastery's most famous feature: the rare *Ati-Buddha (Adi-Buddha) statue in the Yab-Yum position*. This posture symbolizes the union of male and female principles in Tantric Buddhism and is uncommon in the region.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Historical Founding Records (1730 AD) by Ngadakpa Lama
description: Documents related to the monastery's establishment in *1730 A.D.* by *Ngadakpa Lama, confirming its status as the **third oldest monastery in Sikkim*. Includes information on its historical monastic strength (reported 'around 98 lamas') and regional significance.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Architectural Survey and Environmental Context
description: Documentation of the traditional Himalayan Buddhist architectural style. Includes images of the stone steps/mountain trail access and the monastery's tranquil setting amidst forested hills near the *Varsey Rhododendron Sanctuary*, emphasizing the panoramic Himalayan views.

title: Gonjang Monastery: Historical Foundation & Nyingma Jangter Lineage Documents (1981)
description: Founding history by H.E. *Tingkye Gonjang Rinpoche* (1981) and its connection to the 15th-century *Tertön* lineage of Yolmo Terton Ngakchang Shakya Zangpo. Documents detail the continuity of the *'hidden-treasure' (Jangter) tradition* in modern Sikkim. Includes reference to the 19th-century Tibetan precursor, 'Gonjang Samten Choephug'.

title: Gonjang Monastery: Iconography and Mural Catalogue - Main Shrine Hall
description: High-resolution digital archive of the large *Buddha statue, the statues of the **Khen-lop Cho-Sum* (Three Founding Fathers of Tibetan Buddhism), and the engraved statues of the *Je-Bang Nyernga* (Twenty-Five Disciples of Padmasambhava). Detailed images of the intricate and colourful interior murals and door-frames.

description: Catalogue of *unique religious artefacts* housed at the monastery, including probable ritual implements (*vajra, bell, dorje, phurba*), specialized thangka paintings, prayer wheels, and other objects specific to the Nyingma Order's tradition.

description: Curriculum details for the *nine-year monastic course* covering Tibetan language, English, moral values, and comparative study of *Indian and Tibetan Buddhist philosophy. Includes operational records of the **Thadrol Dorjee Memorial Community Health Care Centre* which serves monks and local community members.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Rare Ati-Buddha Statue Documentation (Yab-Yum Position)
description: Detailed study and high-resolution imagery of the monastery's most famous feature: the rare *Ati-Buddha (Adi-Buddha) statue in the Yab-Yum position*. This posture symbolizes the union of male and female principles in Tantric Buddhism and is uncommon in the region.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Historical Founding Records (1730 AD) by Ngadakpa Lama
description: Documents related to the monastery's establishment in *1730 A.D.* by *Ngadakpa Lama, confirming its status as the **third oldest monastery in Sikkim*. Includes information on its historical monastic strength (reported 'around 98 lamas') and regional significance.

title: Rinchenpong Monastery: Architectural Survey and Environmental Context
description: Documentation of the traditional Himalayan Buddhist architectural style. Includes images of the stone steps/mountain trail access and the monastery's tranquil setting amidst forested hills near the *Varsey Rhododendron Sanctuary*, emphasizing the panoramic Himalayan views.

title: Kewzing Bon Monastery: Unique Bon Tradition & Founding Documents (c. 1980 CE)
description: Founding history of Kewzing Bon Monastery (c. 1980 CE), noted as one of the only Bon monasteries in Sikkim and India. Documents detailing its distinct lineage from mainstream Tibetan Buddhism and its role in preserving the *Bon tradition* in the region.

title: Kewzing Bon Monastery: Iconography and Ritual Art (Bon Deities)
description: Catalogue and visual documentation of the interior paintings and unique iconography, specifically featuring the *Bon Demonical Buddha* and *Bon Deities*. Documentation of Bon ritual implements (drums, conch) and how they differ from Nyingma/Kagyu traditions.

title: Kewzing Bon Monastery: Educational Outreach and Rural Community Integration
description: Records documenting the monastery's educational institution that offers *Bon teachings to young students belonging to less privileged families. Documentation of the monastery's role in the **Kewzing Village tourism model* and its contribution to local culture.

title: Rumtek Monastery: Historical Foundation and Dharma Chakra Centre Establishment (1966)
description: Documents related to the *Original Founding* (1734 AD) by the 9th Karmapa, and the *Re-establishment* in 1966 by the 16th Karmapa, Rangjung Rigpe Dorje, as the seat-in-exile and *Dharma Chakra Centre*. Includes documentation on its near-replica design of Tsurphu Monastery (Tibet).

title: Rumtek Monastery: Relics and Golden Stupa of the 16th Karmapa
description: Detailed inventory and visual documentation of the *Golden Stupa* containing the relics of the *16th Karmapa*. Catalogue of precious objects and rare religious art (including statues and silk paintings) brought from Tsurphu Monastery in Tibet, making it an international repository.

title: Rumtek Monastery: Karma Shri Nalanda Institute for Higher Buddhist Studies Curriculum
description: Detailed curriculum of the *9-year advanced course* offered by the *Karma Shri Nalanda Institute*, covering Buddhist philosophy, logic, meditation, and debate within the Karma Kagyu lineage. Includes information on monastic scholarship and its role in disseminating teachings.

title: Enchey Monastery: Founding Legend and Historical Significance (Lama Drupthob Karpo)
description: Founding legend associated with the tantric master *Lama Drupthob Karpo* (who established a hermitage here). Documents covering the site's Nyingma lineage and its historical role as a spiritual seat near *Gangtok, with major rebuilding dating to around **1909 AD*.

title: Enchey Monastery: Detor Cham Mask Dance and Annual Festival Documentation
description: Documentation of the annual *Detor Cham (Mask Dance)* festival held in January/February (18th & 19th day of the 12th lunar month). Includes records of other important festivals like *Pang Lhabsol* (commemoration of the blood-brotherhood between Bhutias & Lepchas).

title: Enchey Monastery: Nyingma Murals, Art, and Architectural Survey
description: Architectural survey of the hill-top structure, noting its proximity to Gangtok (3 km). Includes a catalogue of the rich wall murals depicting deities, *wrathful gods, and the **Four Guardian Kings. Inventory of the **ritual masks* used in the Cham dances.

title: Tashiding Monastery: Founding Legend and Sacred Site Status (8th Century Onward)
description: Documents detailing the site's sanctification by *Guru Padmasambhava* (8th century) and the establishment of the present structure (c. *1717 AD) by the 3rd Chogyal, Chakdor Namgyal. Emphasizes its status as **'The Devoted Central Glory'* and one of the holiest Nyingma sites in Sikkim.

title: Tashiding Monastery: Bhumchu Festival and Holy Vase (Bumchu) Documentation
description: Detailed documentation of the annual *Bhumchu (Holy Water) festival* (Feb/March). Includes records of the ritual opening of the *Sacred Vase (Bumchu)* for making *prophecies* about the coming year, underscoring its role in Nyingma *Terma (hidden-treasure) tradition*.

title: Tashiding Monastery: Architectural Survey of Chortens and Mani Stone Fields
description: Survey of the expansive compound located between the Rathong Chu and Rangeet rivers. Includes documentation of the *41 Chortens* (categorized as 'Chortens of Enlightenment' etc.) and the approach paths lined with carved *Mani stone slabs*.

title: Sanga Choeling Monastery: Founding History and Nyingma Retreat Status (c. 1701 AD)
description: Founding history (c. 1701 AD) by *Lhatsün Namkha Jikmé (Lhatsun Chempo), making it one of Sikkim's oldest monasteries. The name translates to **'Island of Secret Mantra Teachings'*, highlighting its historical role as a secluded Nyingma retreat and meditation center above Pelling.

title: Sanga Choeling Monastery: Ancient Clay Idols and Art Conservation
description: Catalogue and condition report of ancient (possibly *17th-century) clay idols, statues of Padmasambhava, Sakyamuni Buddha, and Vajrasattva. Documentation of wall paintings, including the **'Wheel of Life'* motif, emphasizing the preservation challenges due to damage (fire, earthquake).

title: Sanga Choeling Monastery: Architectural Survey and Ridge-Top Setting
description: Architectural documentation of the traditional timber and stone structure, emphasizing its dramatic *ridge-top location* (accessible via a steep uphill forest walk from Pelling). Includes panoramic views and the site's role as a serene viewpoint for Himalayan peaks.

title: Phodong Monastery: Karma Kagyu Founding and Historical Reconstruction (1740 AD)
description: Founding history (c. *1740 AD) under the **4th Chogyal, Gyurmed Namgyal, establishing it as an early major center of the **Karma Kagyu lineage* in Sikkim. Includes records of the significant reconstruction in *1977* and its status as one of Sikkim's six most important monasteries.

title: Phodong Monastery: Cham Dance Festival and Large Monastic Community Documentation
description: Documentation of the annual *Cham Dance festival* held on the 28th and 29th day of the 10th Tibetan calendar month (Dec/Jan). Records detailing the large resident monastic community (historically around *260 monks*), confirming its role as a major monastic training center.

title: Phodong Monastery: Ancient Murals and Frescoes Catalogue
description: Catalogue of the beautiful and intricate ancient *murals and frescoes* adorning the walls of the main prayer hall. Documentation of the monastery's Karma Kagyu iconographic features and its vibrant colour façade, positioned on a hillside ridge near Gangtok (38 km).
